methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Field AI, founded by Ali Agha and Shayegan Omidshafiei, is an AI & Software company at IPO stage with a current valuation of $2.0B and $405M total raised. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 8 private-sector peers in similar AI & Software positioning and valuation tier, with 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR). The peer set spans stage diversity from Series A through Series E, reflecting the heterogeneous funding environment in frontier AI.

Peer-median triangulation, the applicable method given available data, yielded $5.7B as the central implied valuation. This median is anchored by a peer range of $1.0B to $10.0B. Sample peers include Crusoe Energy ($10.0B, Series E), Wayve ($8.6B, Series D), Together AI ($8.3B, Series A), SandboxAQ ($5.8B, Series E), and Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B). The $5.7B median reflects clustering around mid-to-high frontier valuations within the AI software category.

The triangulated range supports a valuation band of $4.8B to $6.5B, with $5.7B as the median point—substantially above Field AI's current $2.0B mark. This gap likely reflects either material progress post-most-recent funding or market repricing of AI infrastructure assets toward IPO. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, competitive differentiation relative to the broader peer cohort, and public market appetite for unprofitable AI software companies at IPO pricing.
